headers.//: Firebase Auth reserved paths (/__/auth/*, /__/firebase/*) MUST be excluded from our app CSP. Those pages are served by Firebase Hosting and contain inline scripts/iframes that handle the OAuth flow. Applying our default-src 'self' + script-src without 'unsafe-inline' breaks the auth handler entirely (manifests as auth/internal-error on desktop popup, blank page after sign-in tap on mobile redirect). We keep nosniff and HSTS as low-risk basics; CSP/X-Frame-Options/Permissions-Policy are omitted so Firebase's own headers apply.

frame-src includes 'self' so Firebase Auth's hidden iframe at ${authDomain}/__/auth/iframe (used by signInWithPopup AND signInWithRedirect to coordinate the OAuth round-trip) is allowed. With authDomain pinned to the runtime host, that iframe is same-origin — without 'self' here, every sign-in throws auth/internal-error.
headers.value: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://apis.google.com https://*.googleapis.com https://*.gstatic.com; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' https://fonts.googleapis.com; font-src https://fonts.gstatic.com; connect-src 'self' https://*.googleapis.com https://*.firebaseio.com https://api.fda.gov wss://*.firebaseio.com https://identitytoolkit.googleapis.com https://securetoken.googleapis.com; img-src 'self' data: https://*.googleusercontent.com https://firebasestorage.googleapis.com https://*.google.com; frame-src 'self' https://accounts.google.com https://*.firebaseapp.com https://*.googleapis.com
